subject: Geography
title: Upside-Down Maps
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Upside-Down Maps.

hook.text: There is no scientific reason north is at the top of maps. It's just a convention.

buildup.text: Early Islamic maps placed south at the top. Chinese maps sometimes put west up.

discovery.text: The convention of 'north up' only became standard after European colonial-era mapmaking.

twist.text: Australian 'Corrective Maps' place south at the top, flipping the visual hierarchy.

climax.text: Orientation shapes perception. Placing a country at the top implies importance.

punchline.text: North is only 'up' because someone decided it was.

quiz.question: Why is north at the top of most maps?
options: It became convention during European colonial mapmaking
North is physically higher than south
